data.title: Jakob's Law
data.description: Users spend most of their time on other sites, so they prefer user interfaces that work similarly to those they already know.
data.technical_spec: UI Consistency: Familiar navigation patterns; Accessibility: Standard icons and gestures

data.title: Fitts's Law
data.description: The time to acquire a target is a function of the distance to and size of the target.
data.technical_spec: Design: Large touch targets for buttons; Spacing: Adequate margins to prevent misclicks

data.title: Hick's Law
data.description: The time it takes to make a decision increases with the number and complexity of choices.
data.technical_spec: Decision Points: Simplified choices; UI Flow: Minimalism in design

data.title: Miller's Law
data.description: The average person can only keep about seven items in their working memory.
data.technical_spec: Content Design: Chunking information; Interface: Clear categorization of messages

data.title: Peak-End Rule
data.description: People judge an experience largely based on how they felt at its peak and end.
data.technical_spec: Experience Design: Focused on user interaction points; User Feedback: Positive end experiences
